verses.text: Asked where her beloved has gone, she answers that he has gone to his garden, and declares, 'I am my beloved's, and my beloved is mine.' Their belonging is unshaken. The soul rests in the assurance of mutual covenant with Christ.

Asked where her beloved has gone, she answers that he has gone to his garden, and declares, 'I am my beloved's, and my beloved is mine.' Their belonging is unshaken. The soul rests in the assurance of mutual covenant with Christ.

Asked where her beloved has gone, she answers that he has gone to his garden, and declares, 'I am my beloved's, and my beloved is mine.' Their belonging is unshaken. The soul rests in the assurance of mutual covenant with Christ.
He praises her beauty, comely as Jerusalem, and overcome, says, 'Turn away thine eyes from me.' Her loveliness moves him deeply. Christ is delighted by the faith and devotion of His people.

Among many queens his dove is 'but one,' the only one of her mother, fair 'as the morning... clear as the sun.' She is uniquely his. The Church is Christ's one beloved, radiant with borrowed glory.

Among many queens his dove is 'but one,' the only one of her mother, fair 'as the morning... clear as the sun.' She is uniquely his. The Church is Christ's one beloved, radiant with borrowed glory.

He goes down to the garden to see its growth, and the bride is called back as the longed-for Shulamite. Love seeks and is sought. Christ tends His garden and calls His people to be seen and admired in Him.
